title: Prompt Wording Sensitivity
concept: Small changes in instruction wording can significantly alter outputs due to token embedding differences. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Context Window Management.'

exercises.prompt: When you ask an AI a question, the answer...

options.text: Can change depending on the exact words you use
Is completely random every single second

exercises.explain: Even small changes in phrasing can lead to different AI responses because of how the AI processes words mathematically.

cards.title: Tiny Words, Huge Changes
cards.body: Have you ever noticed how asking a friend to "help" vs. "do it for me" gets a totally different reaction? AI is the same way! A single word change can completely flip the kind of answer you get. Let's find out why!

cards.title: What is Wording Sensitivity?
cards.body: Prompt Wording Sensitivity means that even tiny changes in your instructions can cause the AI to give completely different answers. This happens because the AI translates every word into mathematical coordinates (called token embeddings), and small changes shift those coordinates!

cards.title: The Train Track Switch
cards.body: Imagine an AI is like a train track switcher. Changing just one tiny word in your prompt is like flipping a small switch on the tracks. Even though it's a minor change at the start, it sends the train down a completely different path, arriving at a totally different destination!

cards.title: A One-Word Difference
cards.body: If you ask an AI: "Write a short story," you might get a simple tale. But if you change just one word to: "Compose a short story," the AI might use much more poetic and formal language, because "compose" triggers different associations in its brain than "write."

cards.title: Myth: AI Knows What You Mean
cards.body: Many people think that AIs are smart enough to know what you "mean," so word choice doesn't matter. In reality, AIs don't think like humans. They rely on exact math, so even swapping "make" for "create" can alter the output.

cards.title: Visualizing the Shift
cards.body: See how changing just one word shifts the mathematical direction (token embedding) and output of the AI!

exercises.prompt: What can happen if you change a single word in your prompt, like switching "quick" to "fast"?

options.text: The AI will always ignore the change.
It can significantly alter the AI's output.
The AI will crash and show an error.
It makes the AI run much faster.

exercises.explain: Because of token embedding differences, even tiny adjustments to spelling or vocabulary can push the AI's mathematical calculations in a new direction.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Using the word "explain" instead of "describe" can change the type of answer the AI generates.
exercises.explain: Even if two words mean similar things to us, the AI sees them as different mathematical tokens, which can change the final answer.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to explain why word choice matters to an AI.
exercises.sentence: Small changes in your prompt alter the token __________, causing the AI to generate different outputs.
exercises.explain: Words are converted into numbers called token embeddings. Changing a word changes these numbers, leading the AI down a different path.

exercises.prompt: Which pair of prompts shows how a single word change can guide the AI to two different styles of response?

options.text: Prompt A: "Give a brief summary." Prompt B: "Give a detailed summary."
Prompt A: "Explain code." Prompt B: "Explain code."

exercises.explain: Prompt A uses the word "brief," which guides the AI's math toward short summaries. Prompt B uses "detailed," directing it to write a long explanation. Prompt C doesn't make sense as a prompt.

exercises.prompt: Why does changing a single word in a prompt sometimes give you a completely different AI response?

options.text: AIs ignore small words like 'not' or 'never' entirely.
Tiny changes in prompt words alter token embeddings, changing the AI's path.
The AI always gives the same answer if the topic is the same.
AIs only read the very first word of your instruction.

exercises.explain: Even tiny shifts in how you word a prompt can change the way the AI's mathematical brain connects your words, leading to very different answers. Up next, we'll learn how to manage the amount of text an AI can remember at once: Context Window Management!
